skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 1.5 Pro, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound. Use for: social media videos, marketing content, explainer videos, product demos, AI avatars. Triggers: video generation, ai video, text to video, image to video, veo, animate image, video from image, ai animation, video generator, generate video, t2v, i2v, ai video maker, create video with ai, runway alternative, pika alternative, sora alternative, kling alternative
skill.summary: Generate AI videos with 40+ models including Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, and Grok via inference.sh CLI for text-to-video, image-to-video, avatar animation, and video upscaling.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 48 external command patterns and 16 hardcoded URLs, but all findings are false positives. The skill is documentation-only (SKILL.md) that provides examples of legitimate inference.sh CLI usage for AI video generation. Commands are constrained to documented infsh app run patterns. No user input injection or malicious patterns detected.

low_findings.title: External CLI dependency
low_findings.description: Skill requires inference.sh CLI (infsh) installation. Commands like infsh app run execute via bash with user-provided prompts and image URLs.

content.value_statement: Creating professional AI videos typically requires multiple tools and subscriptions. This skill provides unified access to 40+ video generation models through a single CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text prompts using Google Veo 3.1, Seedance, Wan, and Grok models
Animate static images into videos with image-to-video models
Create AI avatars with lip-sync using OmniHuman and Fabric models
Upscale low-resolution videos using Topaz Video Upscaler
Add sound effects to silent videos with HunyuanVideo Foley
Merge multiple video clips with transition effects
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication (infsh login)
Video generation depends on external API availability and rate limits
Generated video quality and duration vary by model selection
Cannot generate videos locally - all processing occurs on inference.sh platform

use_cases.title: Social media content creation
use_cases.description: Generate engaging short-form videos for platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts from simple text descriptions or existing images.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Product demo videos
use_cases.description: Create professional product demonstration videos with AI avatars explaining features, ideal for marketing and e-commerce without filming equipment.

use_cases.title: Educational content
use_cases.description: Produce explainer videos and animated tutorials by converting educational scripts into visual content with narration and animations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic text-to-video generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video showing a drone shot flying over a forest at sunset. Use Google Veo 3.1 Fast for quick results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick video generation from a simple description

prompt_templates.title: Image animation with specific model
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this landscape image to show moving clouds and flowing water. Use Wan 2.5 for image-to-video conversion. Image URL: [insert URL]

prompt_templates.title: AI avatar with narration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking avatar video using this portrait image and audio narration. Use OmniHuman 1.5 for multi-character support. Portrait URL: [insert URL], Audio URL: [insert URL]

prompt_templates.title: Complete video production workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a complete video workflow: First, generate a 10-second intro scene of a futuristic city using Veo 3.1. Then create an AI avatar explaining our product using this script [insert script] and this photo [insert URL]. Finally, merge both clips with a fade transition and add background sound effects of city ambience.
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-step video production combining generation, avatar, and editing

output_examples.input: Generate a timelapse video of a flower blooming in a garden
output_examples.output: Video generated successfully using google/veo-3-1-fast. Duration: 8 seconds. Resolution: 1280x720. Output URL: https://inference.sh/output/abc123.mp4

output_examples.input: Create a talking avatar from this portrait with the provided audio narration
output_examples.output: Avatar video created using bytedance/omnihuman-1-5. The portrait has been animated with synchronized lip movements matching the audio. Duration: 15 seconds. Output URL: https://inference.sh/output/xyz789.mp4

best_practices: Start with shorter prompt descriptions and iterate - detailed prompts do not always produce better results
Choose models based on your needs: Veo for quality, P-Video for speed, Wan for image animation
Keep image URLs publicly accessible and in supported formats (JPG, PNG) for image-to-video generation
anti_patterns: Do not include sensitive or private information in video prompts as they are processed externally
Avoid overly long prompts - the AI may not process all details effectively
Do not expect photorealistic results from all models - quality varies significantly between models

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools to install. Then authenticate with infsh login. The skill will guide you through setup if not already installed.

faq.question: What is the difference between Veo 3.1 and Veo 3.1 Fast?
faq.answer: Veo 3.1 produces higher quality videos with frame interpolation but takes longer. Veo 3.1 Fast prioritizes speed and is suitable for quick iterations and prototyping.

faq.question: Can I generate videos without an inference.sh account?
faq.answer: No. An inference.sh account and authentication are required. The CLI needs valid credentials to access the video generation models.

faq.question: What video formats are supported for image-to-video?
faq.answer: Input images should be JPG or PNG format. Output videos are typically MP4. Some models support specific resolutions like 480p, 720p, or 1080p depending on the model.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Generation time varies by model and video length. Fast models may complete in 30-60 seconds, while high-quality models can take several minutes. The CLI provides real-time progress updates.

faq.question: Can I use my own audio for avatar videos?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide a publicly accessible URL to your audio file (MP3 format recommended) when using avatar models like OmniHuman or Fabric. The avatar will lip-sync to your audio.
